title: ageratina ciliata
intro: Ageratina ciliata, also known as Mexican Sage or Hierba de la Vibora, is a perennial plant native to Mexico and Central America. It is a member of the mint family and is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The plant is used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Ageratina ciliata is traditionally prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to soothe digestive issues, relieve fever, and promote overall well-being.
